parsed.advice_text:
**please call 911 or go to your nearest emergency room right now.**

fast breathing combined with bluish lips (called cyanosis) can be a sign that your toddler isn't getting enough oxygen — that's an emergency that needs immediate medical evaluation, not something to wait on. don't drive yourself if you're too panicked; calling 911 means trained help starts on the way immediately. while you wait, keep your toddler calm and upright if possible, and stay on the line with the dispatcher — they'll guide you through what to do next.

framework_citations.reference: AAP — When to Call 911 / Pediatric Emergency Warning Signs
framework_citations.excerpt: Bluish color of the lips or fingernails (cyanosis) and rapid or labored breathing are listed as emergency warning signs requiring immediate medical attention.

framework_citations.reference: CPS — Caring for Kids: When to Go to the Emergency Department
framework_citations.excerpt: Blue or purple lips or fingertips and breathing faster than normal are signs to seek emergency care immediately.

follow_up_questions: Has he been sick recently with a cough, fever, or cold symptoms?
Did he put anything in his mouth just before this started?
Is he making any unusual sounds while breathing, like wheezing or a high-pitched noise?
